v.n. To begin to sing; to sing.
adj. Quarrelsome, pugnacious, turbulent.
(v.n. fr. روح 'to become sprightly,' &c.), s.m.(?), Wine; — enjoying satisfaction (cf. next).

def: s.f. Road, way, path, passage; journey, progress; means of access, access; manner, method; custom, fashion; — postpn. By way (of, -kī); in the path (of), for the sake (of = rāh-meṅ)

(-kī), To stop the path or progress (of), to refuse admittance (to), to bar out

(-ko), To put one on the road (which had been lost); to guide (one) in the right path, to reform
To make a way or path; to establish relations (with), form an acquaintance or friendship (with)

'To seize the skirt of a wayfarer's garments,' to pick a quarrel with, quarrel (with one) without just cause
(-kī), To leave the road; to get out of the way, to give way

v.t. To show (one) a road; to make one wait (for)
(-kī), To watch or wait (for), to look (for), expect, await
(-meṅ), To open a way, make way (for, -ko); to grant access (to), to admit

To continue in (one's) usual practice or mode of conduct; to behave properly or according to custom or etiquette
s.m. A traveller, wayfarer; a follower or sectary
s.f. Manners, habits, ways, customs; conduct, behaviour
s.m. Barring the way; keeping the gangway clear
To leave the right road or path, lose (one's) way, go astray; to run to excess, to exceed all bounds
v.n. To act or behave with propriety
To travel a road, get over a road; to quit a path, take a short cut
To delay or linger on the road
s.m. Road, way, passage, channel, pass; — a traveller
To take (one's own) course, follow (one's own) devices
(-kī), To take the way or road (to), to set out (for), to depart; to take (one's) way, take (oneself, apnī) off (e.g. apnī rāh lo)
To rob or plunder on the highway, to waylay; to lay (one) under restraint; to ruin (one's) prospects in life; to play false; to desert
The trace of a path to be effaced: rāh nāpnā, lit. 'To measure the road'; to walk about idly, to employ oneself unprofitably
s.m. A book or map of roads
To open out or make a road; to start or devise anything new; to devise a new mode of conduct
adj. & s.m. Ambling, ambling-paced, quick, active; — an ambling horse, a courser, steed, a good roadster
s.f. = rāh-ravish, q.v. (For other compounds see s.v. rah.)

def: s.f. Road, way, path, &c. ( = rāh, q.v.)

s.f. Going or travelling over a road, traversing a road
s.m. Road, pass, defile; — a wayfarer, traveller
s.m. Wayfarer, &c. ( = rah-guẓār); — adventure, accident; event, occurrence
s.m.f. Wayfarer, traveller; — s.f. The highway; a passage
s.m. Highwayman, &c. ( = rah-zan or rāh-zan, q.v.)
s.m. Traveller, traverser; a galloping or fleet horse; — time (i.e. twice, three times, &c.). For compounds not given here, see s.v. rāh.

def: s.m. corr. of rāhu, q.v. s.m. (Myth.) s.m. Loneliness, solitariness, solitude, retirement, privacy, secrecy; a lonely or deserted place, private or solitary abode, hiding-place; — a secret, mystery, mystical or religious truth; — sexual intercourse, copulation; a privity; pleasantry, merriment, sportive sallies, jocund repartee, waggery; a kind of ballet or theatric representation of Krishn and the Gopīs (a similar entertainment was invented by Wajīd ʻAlī Shāh of Lakhnau and given in his court); — adv. In a lonely or solitary place, in private, in secret, secretly, privately, privily, clandestinely
subs: adv. With merriment, with sportive sallies, merrily, pleasantly, &c.'

def: s.m. Tramp ling, treading under foot (syn. rauṅdan).
v.n. To be pleased, to enjoy oneself, to rejoice, make merry.

adj. & s.m. Secret, private, clandestine, hidden, recondite, mysterious; — a secret, mystery, anything hidden or recondite, secret doctrine, mystical or esoteric teaching; — play or pleasantry, &c. ( = rahas, q.v.).

ety: rah˚ = Prk. रक्ख(इ) = S. रक्ष्य(ते), pass. of rt. रक्ष्; or = S. रह्य(ते), pass. of rt. रह्

def: s.m. King, sovereign, monarch, prince, lord, master, governor; (fig.) an extravagant and careless person.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit राजा (rājā), nominative singular of राजन् (rājan).

def: s.m. = راجا rājā, q.v. s.m. (used in comp.) King, prince, sovereign, chief, master; any principal object, anything the best, or the largest, of its kind; — a master-mason, mason, builder, bricklayer
subs: (˚ja+abh˚), vulg. rāj-abhishek, s.m. Consecration or coronation of a king, royal inauguration or installation (syn. rāj-tilak); — rājājnā, vulg. rājāggyā (˚ja+āj˚), s.f. King's edict, royal decree or command, mandate, ordinance
vulg. rāj-adhirāj (˚ja+adh˚), s.m. King of kings, supreme ruler of princes, paramount sovereign, emperor, mighty potentate
vulg. rāj-adhikār (˚ja+adh˚), s.m. Paramount power; royal prerogative
vulg. rāj-adhikārī, s.m. (f. -inī), 'Royal official'; one who holds sway, regent, governor, ruler, king
(˚ja+āv˚), s.m. A kind of diamond or other gem ( = rāj-paṭṭ, q.v.)
vulg. rājāʼolī (˚ja+āv˚), s.f. Line of kings, royal dynasty or genealogy, royal house ( = rāj-vaṅś or rāj-baṅs)
vulg. raj-bahā, s.m. Principal channel, main distributory, the principal or common branch (of a canal, &c.)
s.m. Royal storehouse; public warehouse; royal exchequer, public treasury; royal kitchen
s.m. Food placed before an idol at noon; endowment of a temple
s.f. A present made by an inferior on admission to the presence of a great man; — fee paid to a public functionary for permission to reap the harvest
s.m. Royal cushion or throne ( = rāj-paṭṭ; see also s.v. rāj, 'government')
vulg. rāj-pat, s.m. 'Lord of kings,' prince (a Hindū title)
s.f. King's wife, royal consort, queen
s.m. King's highway, public road, main road, principal street
s.m. Royal fillet, a tiara; royal cushion, throne; a kind of precious stone, a diamond of inferior quality (said to be brought from Vīrāṭ-deś, in the north-west of India)
s.m. One who accedes to the position and office of king
s.m. and rāj-purī, s.f. = rāj-dhānī, q.v.
s.m. Royal power and splendour, kingly dignity, majesty
s.m. Royal government; royal or public policy; administration

(S. rāja-putra), s.m. (f. -nī), lit. 'King's son or descendant'; name of a military tribe of Hindūs (of the second or Kshatriya caste); name of a class of Hindūs who claim descent from the ancient Kshatriyas; — a member of the Rājpūt caste, a Rājpūt
adj. Like, or belonging to, the Rājpūts; — prop. n. A name for the province of Ajmīr, the country of the Rājpūts
s.f. The essential quality of a Rājpūt, courage, prowess in war
s.m. The king's evil (?); a kind of carbuncle or large boil (which generally rises at the back of the neck and commonly proves fatal)
s.m. The mark placed on the forehead of a royal personage on the occasion of coronation; coronation, installation (cf. rājābhishek); a mark put on the forehead of a royal personage by his vassals
s.m. Royal splendour or dignity, majesty
s.m. The discus of Krishna; — fear or perplexity occasioned by a monarch
adj. (f. -ā), Given or allowed by the king or by Government
s.f. An allowance of land by the king or by Government
s.m. The act of seeing, or appearing before, a king, a royal audience, royal levée
s.f. The royal hall of audience, presence chamber
s.m. 'The act of injuring a king.' high treason, rebellion; — tyranny
adj. & s.m. Rebellious, disloyal; — ʻking-injurer,' traitor, rebel
s.m. King's sceptre, royal sceptre; kingly authority; punishment inflicted by order of a king; tax or fine payable to royalty
(-danḍa+apa˚), s.m. 'Seizure of property in execution of a royal or of a legal decree,' confiscation
s.m. The king's gate, gate of a royal palace, palace-gate, royal entrance
s.f. King's residence, a palace; royal city, seat of government, capital, metropolis
'The king's yoke,' the burden of government, kingly responsibility, royal functions
vulg. rāj-dharam, s.m. A king's duty; the duties of a sovereign, the functions or obligations of government; — duties of the military caste
s.m. Royal revenue or right to property; public property
s.f. Queen-consort, royal spouse; — an epithet of the goddess Kālī
(rāja+r̤ishi), s.m. A royal Rishi or saint; Rishi of royal descent; that holy and superhuman personage which a king or man of the military class may become by the performance of great austerities
s.m. A mortal disease; consumption; (fig.) any great affliction or evil (as a long-pending law-suit, &c.); a mania of any kind (as for building, &c.)
s.f. A royal assembly or court; an assemblage of princes; an assembly in which the king is present, royal council; king's privy council; parliament
vulg. rāj-asthān, s.m. King's palace, &c. ( = rāj-mandir, or rāj-dhānī); — prop. n. Another name for the province of Rājpūtāna
vulg. rāj-sū, s.m. A great sacrifice or religious ceremony performed at the coronation of a supreme sovereign or universal monarch by the king himself and his tributary princes
s.m. Royal possessions: royal revenue, tribute, tax
s.m. Attendant on a royal personage, a king's servant; a courtier
s.m. Royal edict, decree, mandate; king's sway
rāj-kārva, s.m. King's business, royal duties, state affairs, functions of government, public administration
corr. rāj-kār, s.m. Tax paid to royalty, king's taxes, royal tribute, revenue
s.m. The fragrant grass Cyperus rotundus; the root of Cyperus pertenius
s.m. King's family, royal family, royal race or dynasty; king's court; court of justice; — (f. -ā), a king's son, a prince
vulg. rāj-kumbar, rāj-kuṅʼar, rāj-kuṅwar, rāj-kuwar, s.m. A king's son, a prince
and rāj-kanyā, s.f. King's daughter, princess
rāj-gaddī, s.f. Royal cushion or seat, king's throne, royal throne ( = rāj-siṅghāsan)
s.m. Royal counsellor, spiritual preceptor of a king, or prince, or chief
s.m. King's house, royal dwelling, palace
s.m. A species of flat fresh-water fish
rāja-gha, rāja-ghna, s.m. King-killer, a regicide
s.f. Masterly work, workmanship; mason's work, masonry
s.f. Good fortune or glory of a king; — a royal welcome
vulg. rāj-laććhan, s.m. Royal sign or token, any mark (on the body, &c.) indicating a future king; marks of royalty; royal insignia, regalia
s.f. Royal residence, palace, royal apartment
s.f. Royal signet or seal, privy seal
s.m. Minister or counsellor of state, prime-minister, chancellor, privy counsellor
s.f. Senate, cabinet council, privy council; — rāj-mandir, rāj-mandil, s.m. Palace of a king, royal mansion or residence
s.m. = rāj-nīti or nīt, s.f. Royal conduct or policy, administration of government, regal polity, princely duties; science of government; statesmanship; politics; — code of law, jurisprudence
s.m. Country of rājās, royal domains; a kingdom (cf. rajwāṛā)
adj. & adv. Like a king, kingly, royal, princely
s.m. Family of kings, royal race or family, dynasty
(f. -inī), rāj-vaṅśīya, (f. -ā), rāj-vaṅśya (f. -ā), adj. & s.m. Belonging to a royal family, of regal race or descent; — offspring of royalty; a tribe of Rājpūts
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Of royal seed, sprung from a royal race; a royal personage
s.m. Prince of heroes; an epithet of any man of superior bravery
s.f. Royal institute or order, mandate, edict, decree; royal administration ( = rāj-prabandh)
s.m. A species of fish, Cyprinus catla or Niloticus)
rāj-hāṅs, s.m. A goose; a white goose with red legs and bill, the flamingo
s.f. 'King's perverseness'; despotic power, absolute government, despotism, tyranny
s.m. One who makes war against a king, rebel, mutineer, traitor
(˚ja+īś), s.m. King of kings, supreme sovereign; sovereign, prince, governor
(˚ja+in˚), s.m. Lord of Kings, king of kings, supreme sovereign, emperor; powerful king
adj. Befitting a king, suitable for royalty; kingly, princely, royal.

def: s.f. Hope, expectation; prayer, supplication, intercession.
wety: From Arabic رَجَاء (rajāʔ).

def: part. adj. Filled, sated, satisfied, having a bellyful (syn. shikam-ser)
subs: adj. Full, sated, &c. ( = rajā); rich, wealthy
wety: From Arabic رَجَاء (rajāʔ).

def: s.f. corr. of رضا raẓā, q.v. s.f. The state of being pleased, content, &c.; pleasure, good pleasure, will; content; approval; acquiescence, consent, assent; permission, leave, furlough
subs: s.f. List of holy-days, feasts, &c. in the course of the year (on which days public offices are closed)
s.m. One who seeks the goodwill or pleasure, or the gratification (of another)
s.f. Seeking the goodwill or pleasure or approbation (of), effort to please
adj. Willing, wishing, consenting, acquiescing, compliant; permitting (cf. rāẓī)
s.f. Good pleasure, willingness; consent; satisfaction; compliance; permission
s.f. Free will and pleasure, free consent
adv. Willingly, of (one's) free will and consent.
wety: From Arabic رَجَاء (rajāʔ).

ety: v.n. fr. رجب 'to fear; to venerate'
def: s.m. The seventh month of the Arabian and Moḥammadan year (so called because of the honour in which it was held in the Time of Ignorance, inasmuch as war or fighting during it was held unlawful)
subs: s.m. 'Rajab the honoured,' the month Rajab.

def: s.f. = rājāʼī, q.v. Kingship, royalty, rank or function of a king, sovereignty, princedom, kingly dignity or authority, supremacy, government, sway, rule, reign.
s.f. See رضائي raẓāʼī, q.v.
s.f. Enjoyment of sovereignty, sovereignty, rule; royal decree, edict, mandate, order, command

ety: rājā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: Kingship, royalty, rank or function of a king, sovereignty, princedom, kingly dignity or authority, supremacy, government, sway, rule, reign.
s.m. (f. -ī), A washerman.
s.f. A washerman's wife; a washerwoman; — epithet of a woman on the third day of the menses.
adj. Kingly, royal, princely.
s.f. = next, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. رمض 'to become intensely heated or hot'
def: s.m. The ninth of the Arabian months (so called because, when they changed the names of the months from the ancient language, they named them according to the seasons in which they fell, and this month agreed with the days of vehement heat. On the 27th of this month the Qorʼān began to descend; and every prayer offered up on that night will, it is believed, be complied with; also, prayers offered up on the 19th, 21st, and 23rd days of this month are supposed to be peculiarly efficacious); — the fast observed during this month, the Moḥammadan Lent (during which Moḥammadans are interdicted from eating, drinking, and conjugal duty, between the morning dawn and appearance of the stars at night).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَمَضَان (ramazān), from Arabic رَمَضَان (ramaḍān).
